mechanism_narrative: A consistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market, which supports strong consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are flat or negative in more than 60% of periods where UNRATE remains below 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a critical threshold, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle, which increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, thereby reducing corporate profitability and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0% for at least two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based strength in consumer demand and investment, which collectively drives corporate earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month avg growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month avg growth > 1.0%
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat when both RSAFS and HOUST show sustained strong growth as per the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a sustained increase in industrial production signals a robust rebound in manufacturing and supply chain activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth above 0.3% over three months does not correlate with positive S&P 500 returns during identified recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth signifies a strong overall economic environment, which boosts corporate profits and investor confidence, typically leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat despite sustained GDP growth above the 2.0% threshold for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, which negatively impacts equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite high year-over-year inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Robust non-farm payrolls growth signifies a healthy job market, which directly increases consumer income and spending, thereby supporting corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative or flat following strong payrolls reports (change > 150,000) in more than 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and an increasing federal funds rate signals a severe economic downturn or stagflationary environment where monetary policy is tightening into a weakening labor market, severely impacting corporate earnings and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive or flat when both unemployment rises significantly and the federal funds rate is elevated as per the trigger condition.
